zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Jackson於1621年7月出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親Michael "Thomas" Jackson56歲，母親Anne Hobson40歲。她於1639年9月30日與John Hudson在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格德曼徹斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1668年8月在英國英格蘭享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Jackson于1621年7月出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，她的父亲Michael "Thomas" Jackson56岁，母亲Anne Hobson40岁。她于1639年9月30日与John Hudson在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾格德曼徹斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1668年8月在英国英格兰享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Jackson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: patronymic from Jack . In North America, this surname has absorbed other patronymics beginning with J- in various European languages, in particular those derived from equivalents or short forms and other derivatives of the personal name Jacob , e.g. Norwegian Jacobsen or Jakobsen and, in some cases, Slovenian Jakše (from a derivative of the personal name Jakob ). This surname is also very common among African Americans (see also 2 below).

African American: from the personal name Jackson (or Andrew Jackson), adopted in honor of Andrew Jackson, the 7th president of the US; or adoption of the surname in 1 above, in many cases probably for the same reason.

History: This extremely common British name was brought over by numerous different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One forebear was the father and namesake of the seventh US president, Andrew Jackson, who migrated to SC from Carrickfergus in the north of Ireland in 1765. The Confederate General Thomas ‘Stonewall’ Jackson came from VA, where his great-grandfather John, likewise of Scotch–Irish stock, had settled after emigrating to America in 1748.

variants.name: Jack
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Jak, Jakke, Jagge, Jake, Jeke, Jegge, a Picard-Flemish denasalized form of Old Picard and Middle Dutch Janke, a pet form of Jan (see John ). It was introduced by Flemings and Picards into Norman and Anglo-Norman usage, whence it became a common English and Scottish pet form of John. Although the surname is mainly Scottish in distribution, it also occurs in England, though the more common form there is Jackson .

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English borrowing of the Old French personal name Jacque(s) (James). However, it is uncertain whether English Jack was ever used as an alternative to James.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Jack (see 1 above) as a surname.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Jackson was born in July 1621, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, Michael "Thomas" Jackson, was 56 and her mother, Anne Hobson, was 40. She married John Hudson on 30 September 1639, in Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in August 1668, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Jackson est née en juillet 1621, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Michael "Thomas" Jackson, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Anne Hobson, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé John Hudson le 30 septembre 1639, à Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en août 1668, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Jackson è nata il nel luglio 1621 nel London, Middlesex, England, sua padre, Michael "Thomas" Jackson, aveva 56 anni e sua madre, Anne Hobson, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato John Hudson il 30 settembre 1639, nel Godmanchester, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel agosto 1668, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Jackson(이)가 1621년 7월 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Michael "Thomas" Jackson(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Hobson(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1639년 9월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 고드먼체스터에서 John Hudson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1668년 8월 영국 잉글랜드에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Jackson родилась июль 1621 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Michael "Thomas" Jackson было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Hobson, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hudson 30 сентября 1639 г. в местности годменчестир, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла август 1668 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет.
